natural_language: The ultimate challenge — nine blocks, three separate three-block towers. Tower Alpha from bottom: A on table, B on A, C on top. Tower Beta: D on table, E on D, F on top. Tower Gamma: G on table, H on G, I crowning the peak. Only C, F, and I are exposed (one per tower). Hand empty. Your mission: completely dissolve all three towers and recombine them into two brand-new towers. New Tower One from the table up: A at the base, then C on A, then F on C, then I at the summit. So it uses blocks from all three original towers. New Tower Two: B at the base, then D on B, then E on D, then G on E, then H at the peak. Notice how original relationships are severed — C no longer sits on B, F no longer sits on E, I no longer sits on H. Everything shifts. Nine blocks, two towers, complete reshuffle.
